title: Cruise and Ballistic Missiles
focus: Conceptual differences between cruise and ballistic missiles, including path, range, payload, and interception characteristics.
rationale: This is a foundational concept corner comparing two major missile types, ideal for statement-based conceptual questions in UPSC.
sectionExcerpt: Cruise Missile: Powered by jet engines for continuous flight throughout their journey... Flies at relatively low altitudes (within the earth's atmosphere)... Ballistic Missile: Rocket-powered for the initial boost phase, then coast unpowered for most of their flight... Follows a ballistic trajectory, arcing upward into the atmosphere before descending... Quasi ballistic missiles are a class of missiles that have low trajectory and take ballistic trajectory but can change course and make maneuvers during the flight if required.
evidenceBullets: Cruise missiles are powered by jet engines for continuous flight and fly at low altitudes within the atmosphere.
Ballistic missiles are rocket-powered initially, then coast unpowered in a ballistic trajectory arcing into the atmosphere.
Cruise missiles are harder to detect but easier to intercept after detection, whereas ballistic missiles are easier to detect early but harder to intercept due to speed.
Quasi ballistic missiles follow a low ballistic trajectory but can maneuver and change course during flight.

title: Agni-5 Missile and Strategic Forces Command
focus: Specifications of the Agni-5 missile, MIRV technology, and the role of the Strategic Forces Command (SFC).
rationale: Agni-5's successful test with MIRV capability is a major strategic milestone, and SFC's role is a prime target for polity and defense questions.
sectionExcerpt: India successfully test-fired Agni-5 Intermediate-Range Ballistic Missile. The launch was carried out under the Strategic Forces Command (SFC). SFC forms part of India's Nuclear Command Authority and is responsible for the management and administration of the country's tactical and strategic nuclear weapons stockpile. About Agni-5: A Nuclear-capable Surface-to-Surface Ballistic Missile (SSBM) and is powered by a three-stage solid fuel engine. Range: Over 5,000 km... Places India among few nations (US, Russia, China, France) with Multiple Independently Targetable Reentry Vehicle (MIRV)-capable ICBMs.
evidenceBullets: Agni-5 is a nuclear-capable Surface-to-Surface Ballistic Missile with a range of over 5,000 km.
It is powered by a three-stage solid fuel engine.
Agni-5 features Multiple Independently Targetable Reentry Vehicle (MIRV) capability.
Strategic Forces Command (SFC) is part of the Nuclear Command Authority and manages India's nuclear weapons stockpile.

title: IGMDP and Agni Prime Missile
focus: Missiles developed under the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP) and the features of the new-generation Agni Prime.
rationale: IGMDP is a classic UPSC topic requiring candidates to match missiles to their types, while Agni Prime represents next-generation advancements like canister-launch and rail-based mobility.
sectionExcerpt: About IGMDP: Started in 1983 to attain self-sufficiency in the field of missile technology. The missiles developed under the program were: Prithvi: Short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile. Trishul: Short-range low-level surface-to-air missile. Akash: Medium-range surface-to-air missile. Nag: Third-generation anti-tank missile. Agni Prime: DRDO & Strategic Force Command (SFC) successfully launched the Agni-Prime Missile from a Rail based Mobile launcher system... Two-stage, solid-fueled, canister-launched ballistic missile. It can carry both conventional and nuclear warheads. First of the new generation of missiles after the end of the IGMDP.
evidenceBullets: IGMDP started in 1983 and includes Prithvi, Trishul, Akash, and Nag missiles.
Prithvi is a surface-to-surface missile, while Trishul and Akash are surface-to-air missiles.
Nag is a third-generation anti-tank missile.
Agni Prime is a two-stage, solid-fueled, canister-launched ballistic missile capable of carrying conventional and nuclear warheads.
Agni Prime utilizes a first-of-its-kind futuristic rail-based mobile launcher system and is the first new generation missile post-IGMDP.
